texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 30.03.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 30.03.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on March 27, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 49.87 lakh ha as compared to 52.35 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 28.50 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text: As on March 27, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 49.87 lakh ha as compared to 52.35 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 28.50 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 27.03.2026, 32.26% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 67% area of Pulses mainly in Gram (66%), Urdbean (86%) & Moong (63%); 71.56% area of Oilseeds majorly in  Rapeseed  & Mustard (75.53%); 38.31% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (54.47%), 25.57% area of Rice and 6.00% area of Wheat  has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 27.03.2026, 32.26% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 67% area of Pulses mainly in Gram (66%), Urdbean (86%) & Moong (63%); 71.56% area of Oilseeds majorly in  Rapeseed  & Mustard (75.53%); 38.31% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (54.47%), 25.57% area of Rice and 6.00% area of Wheat  has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature: Maximum Temperature is likely to be above normal in many parts of Northwest India, many parts of East India, Chhattisgarh, many parts of Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Coastal regions of Tamil Nadu, North Kerala, Coastal Karnataka and Konkan-Goa.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum Temperature is likely to be above normal in many parts of Northwest India, many parts of East India, Chhattisgarh, many parts of Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Coastal regions of Tamil Nadu, North Kerala, Coastal Karnataka and Konkan-Goa.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  March  30  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 71%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 34%; (iii) Central India: (-) 7%; and South Peninsula: 26%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  March  30  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 71%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 34%; (iii) Central India: (-) 7%; and South Peninsula: 26%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from 02nd April, 2026.
texts.text:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from 02nd April, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Wet spell likely to continue over northwest India during the week with peak activity on 29 th & 30 th March and isolated heavy rainfall/snowfall over Kashmir valley on 30th March, 2026.
texts.text:  Wet spell likely to continue over northwest India during the week with peak activity on 29 th & 30 th March and isolated heavy rainfall/snowfall over Kashmir valley on 30th March, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated Hailstorm s likely over Northwest India during 29-30 March, and over Central part of India during 30-31 st March 2026.
texts.text:  Isolated Hailstorm s likely over Northwest India during 29-30 March, and over Central part of India during 30-31 st March 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered to fairly widespread rainfall along with thunderstorm/lightning & gusty wind likely over Northeast  &  adjoining  east  India  during  the  week  with  possibility  of  isolated  heavy  falls  over Northeast India during 30 March-01 April, 2026.
texts.text:  Scattered to fairly widespread rainfall along with thunderstorm/lightning & gusty wind likely over Northeast  &  adjoining  east  India  during  the  week  with  possibility  of  isolated  heavy  falls  over Northeast India during 30 March-01 April, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated rainfall along with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely to continue over South Peninsular India during the week.
texts.text:  Isolated rainfall along with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely to continue over South Peninsular India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Northeast  India,  Jammu  &  Kashmir  adjoining  Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Isolated rainfall activity over Uttarakhand & adjoining plains of western Himalayan region, Central India, Odisha & adjoining Coastal Andhra Pradesh.
texts.text:  Widespread  rainfall  activity  likely  over  Northeast  India,  Jammu  &  Kashmir  adjoining  Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Isolated rainfall activity over Uttarakhand & adjoining plains of western Himalayan region, Central India, Odisha & adjoining Coastal Andhra Pradesh.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status: As on 27 March 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 85.432 billion cubic meter, which is 46.54% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 110.50 percent of last year's storage and 121.03 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status: As on 27 March 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 85.432 billion cubic meter, which is 46.54% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 110.50 percent of last year's storage and 121.03 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels:  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  around  90%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels:  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  around  90%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during post-monsoon 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during post-monsoon 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year Post-monsoon 2024 to Post-monsoon 2025 has shown that about 63% of total analyzed stations of the country experienced rise in annual water level fluctuation may be attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year Post-monsoon 2024 to Post-monsoon 2025 has shown that about 63% of total analyzed stations of the country experienced rise in annual water level fluctuation may be attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in the decadal mean water level fluctuation of 2015-2024 with respect to Post-monsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in the decadal mean water level fluctuation of 2015-2024 with respect to Post-monsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Groundwater Drought Indices indicate that in Post-monsoon 2025 ,  groundwater  conditions across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit . However, localized deficits were observed in several regions - notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadra Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The Groundwater Drought Indices indicate that in Post-monsoon 2025 ,  groundwater  conditions across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit . However, localized deficits were observed in several regions - notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadra Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (19 March - 25 March 2026), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 95%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 25 March 2026) is higher than LPA by 10%.
texts.text:  During the week (19 March - 25 March 2026), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 95%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 25 March 2026) is higher than LPA by 10%.

texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rainfall activity is likely over many parts of Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Gangetic West Bengal and some parts of Bihar, Uttarakhand and Kerala. Rainfall is likely to be above normal over North East India.
texts.text: Rainfall activity is likely over many parts of Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Gangetic West Bengal and some parts of Bihar, Uttarakhand and Kerala. Rainfall is likely to be above normal over North East India.

texts.text:  Maximum Temperature is likely to be above normal over many parts of North West India, East India, Chhattisgarh, Konkan & Goa, Coastal Karnataka, North Kerala, Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam and coastal regions of Tamil Nadu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice: About 28.50 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 30.69 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice: About 28.50 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 30.69 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses: About  6.06  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  5.60  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses: About  6.06  lakh  ha  area  coverage  has  been  reported  compared  to  5.60  lakh  ha  during  the corresponding period of last year.

texts.text: As on 27 March, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 85.432 BCM (46.54%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
